docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
crosszip is a Python utility that makes it easy to apply a function to all possible combinations of elements from multiple iterables. It combines the power of the Cartesian product and functional programming into a single, intuitive tool.

Additionally, crosszip_parametrize Pytest plugin is a decorator that simplifies running tests with all possible combinations of parameter values. This is particularly useful for testing functions or components across multiple dimensions of input parameters.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install crosszip via pip:

pip install crosszip

Registering the crosszip marker for pytest is simple. Just add the following to your pytest.ini file:

[pytest]
markers =
    crosszip_parametrize: "mark test function for crosszip parametrization"

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Example of using crosszip:

# Label Generation for Machine Learning

from crosszip import crosszip

def create_label(category, subcategory, version):
    return f"{category}_{subcategory}_v{version}"

categories = ["cat", "dog"]
subcategories = ["small", "large"]
versions = ["1.0", "2.0"]

labels = crosszip(create_label, categories, subcategories, versions)
print(labels)
# Output: ['cat_small_v1.0', 'cat_small_v2.0', 'cat_large_v1.0', 'cat_large_v2.0', 'dog_small_v1.0', 'dog_small_v2.0', 'dog_large_v1.0', 'dog_large_v2.0']

Example of using pytest marker crosszip_parametrize:

# Testing Power Function

import math
import crosszip

@crosszip_parametrize(
    "base", [2, 10],
    "exponent", [-1, 0, 1],
)
def test_power_function(base, exponent):
    result = math.pow(base, exponent)
    assert result == base ** exponent

For more examples, check out the package documentation at: https://indrajeetpatil.github.io/crosszip/

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Flexible Input: Works with any iterables, including lists, tuples, sets, and generators.
  • pytest Plugin: Supports parametrization of tests using crosszip.
  • Simple API: Minimalist, intuitive design for quick integration into your projects.

docs.title: Using Lists
docs.text:
from crosszip import crosszip

def concat(a, b, c):
    return f"{a}-{b}-{c}"

list1 = [1, 2]
list2 = ['a', 'b']
list3 = [True, False]

result = crosszip(concat, list1, list2, list3)
print(result)
# Output: ['1-a-True', '1-a-False', '1-b-True', '1-b-False', '2-a-True', '2-a-False', '2-b-True', '2-b-False']

docs.title: Using Tuples
docs.text:
from crosszip import crosszip

def add(a, b):
    return a + b

result = crosszip(add, (1, 2), (10, 20))
print(result)
# Output: [11, 21, 12, 22]

docs.title: Using Sets and Generators
docs.text:
from crosszip import crosszip

def concat(a, b):
    return f"{a}{b}"

set1 = {1, 2}
gen = (x for x in ["x", "y"])

result = crosszip(concat, set1, gen)
print(result)
# Output: ['1x', '1y', '2x', '2y']

Danger

Depending on the size of the input iterables, the output of crosszip can grow exponentially. Use with caution when working with large inputs.

docs.title: Label Generation for Machine Learning
docs.text:
from crosszip import crosszip

def create_label(category, subcategory, version):
    return f"{category}_{subcategory}_v{version}"

categories = ["cat", "dog"]
subcategories = ["small", "large"]
versions = ["1.0", "2.0"]

labels = crosszip(create_label, categories, subcategories, versions)
print(labels)
# Output: ['cat_small_v1.0', 'cat_small_v2.0', 'cat_large_v1.0', 'cat_large_v2.0', 'dog_small_v1.0', 'dog_small_v2.0', 'dog_large_v1.0', 'dog_large_v2.0']

docs.title: Generating SQL Query Conditions
docs.text:
from crosszip import crosszip

def create_condition(column, operator, value):
    return f"{column} {operator} {value}"

columns = ["age", "salary"]
operators = [">", "<"]
values = [30, 50000]

conditions = crosszip(create_condition, columns, operators, values)
print(conditions)
# Output: ['age > 30', 'age > 50000', 'age < 30', 'age < 50000', ...]

docs.title: Combining Colors and Textures for Design
docs.text:
from crosszip import crosszip

def create_design(color, texture):
    return f"{color} with {texture} texture"

colors = ["red", "blue"]
textures = ["smooth", "rough"]

designs = crosszip(create_design, colors, textures)
print(designs)
# Output: ['red with smooth texture', 'red with rough texture', 'blue with smooth texture', 'blue with rough texture']

docs.title: crosszip parametrization
docs.text:
Registering the crosszip marker for pytest is simple. Just add the following to your pytest.ini file:

[pytest]
markers =
    crosszip_parametrize: "mark test function for crosszip parametrization"

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
The crosszip_parametrize marker allows you to define parameter names and their corresponding values:

import pytest
from crosszip import crosszip_parametrize

@crosszip_parametrize("a", [1, 2], "b", [3, 4])
def test_example(a, b):
    assert (a, b) in [(1, 3), (1, 4), (2, 3), (2, 4)]

docs.title: Testing Mathematical Functions
docs.text:
import math
from crosszip_parametrize import crosszip_parametrize

@crosszip_parametrize(
    "base", [2, 10],
    "exponent", [-1, 0, 1],
)
def test_power_function(base, exponent):
    result = math.pow(base, exponent)
    assert result == base ** exponent

docs.title: Testing API Endpoints
docs.text:
@crosszip_parametrize(
    "role", ["admin", "user"],
    "http_method", ["GET", "POST", "DELETE"],
)
def test_api_access_control(role, http_method):
    result = simulate_api_call(role=role, method=http_method)
    if role == "admin":
        assert result["status"] == "success"
    else:
        assert result["status"] == "error" if http_method == "DELETE" else "success"

docs.title: Testing Form Validation
docs.text:
@crosszip_parametrize(
    "email", ["valid@example.com", "invalid@", ""],
    "password", ["correct-password", "short", ""],
)
def test_form_validation(email, password):
    result = validate_form(email=email, password=password)
    if "@" not in email or not email:
        assert result["email_error"] == "Invalid email address"
    elif len(password) < 8 or not password:
        assert result["password_error"] == "Password too short"
    else:
        assert result["status"] == "success"

docs.title: Testing Localization Support
docs.text:
@crosszip_parametrize(
    "language", ["en", "es", "fr"],
    "platform", ["web", "mobile"],
)
def test_localized_error_messages(language, platform):
    result = get_localized_message("ERROR_INVALID_INPUT", language, platform)
    if language == "en":
        assert result == "Invalid input"
    elif language == "es":
        assert result == "Entrada no válida"
    elif language == "fr":
        assert result == "Entrée non valide"
